title: SKIP LOCKED Claims, Lease Tokens, Fencing, and Multi-Instance Fairness · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds fenced PostgreSQL fetch-claim protocol from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of fenced PostgreSQL fetch-claim protocol while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Claim a small ordered due batch with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED in one short transaction and return immutable attempt records.
Issue owner, attempt, generation, expiry, and heartbeat budgets so stale or replaced workers cannot finalize.
Finalize only when source, attempt, token, generation, state, and lease match and require exactly one affected row.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Short Claim
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 8275 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove short SKIP LOCKED claims, fenced tokens, affected-row finalize, fairness, and lease faults; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Short Claim.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for claim a small ordered due batch with for update skip locked in one short transaction and return immutable attempt records. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Short Claim, keep Feed Ts Short Claim connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
